title: cleaner formulas in glassbox
summary:
I worked on Glassbox this week to make the formulas simpler. I added coefficient pruning to remove extra terms that don't help. I also added a simplified_ops mode. This turns off logs and exponents, which were making the models too noisy. 

For the math, I added Hoyer regularization and BIC penalty to keep things sparse. I also fixed the MetaArithmetic sigmoid because the gradients were getting stuck. 

Testing showed great results. For y=x^2, the MSE dropped from 0.0810 to 0.0018. It now beats the MLP baseline. The formulas look much cleaner now, mostly because I removed the useless parts. I still need to fix over-pruning for sin(x)+x^2 as it gets a bit too aggressive. Next, I will refine the pruning ratio.
commitMessages: feat(onn): add coefficient pruning + simplified_ops for cleaner formulas
feat(onn): implement Tier 1 quick fixes for improved convergence
